content: Please begin / continue a conversation topic (greetings are not needed). Ensure to change the topic if the current one is losing steam. The conversation should steer towards topics which reveal information about the characters and who they are, or instead drive forward previous conversations in their memory.

**Late visual context as seen by MJR (the player): Alright, folks, gather 'round! I just stumbled upon this wild scene that's gonna make your head spin! So, there's this totally gnarly gamer, MJR, chilling in the game world, right? No, seriously, this is what MJR is seeing right now. Front and center, there's this beautiful lady with flowery hair and a fitted vest. She's squ-sharing a brown sword from her right hip, positioned between her legs. She's catching some sunlight and casting a huge shadow. Wouldn't you know it? She's turned slightly, holding her hand on her hip and standing like she's about to beat you in a fight. These flowers and this ridiculous green atmosphere bring the lighting up, causing the whole tip of her hair to turn orange, though. MJR must think she's the coolest NPC in the game right now. Now, hold onto your seats because this gets even crazier! MJR caught a glimpse of the game's user interface, and holy cow, it's wild! They've got Discord open like it's no big deal, and a bunch of other apps cluttering up the screen. I'm telling you, folks, this game is a real-life saver because you can hook up with the Discord crew while having adventures in the game world. And the UI meter at the bottom of the screen? It's like a game of cat and mouse, trying to keep up with everything at once. So, there you have it, a snapshot from the crazy world of The Elder Scrolls Online. Who knew that MJR managed to capture a moment so crazy in the game world of Tamriel that they have to bring this to our attention too? Stay tuned next time as we take a closer look at our NPC friend and some of the complete nonsense that comes with having Discord open while you are gaming.**

Serana: Look at this glowing shitshow of a forest—feels like Dibella herself pissed moonlight all over the mushrooms just to get us horny in the dark. Bet you'd love to hump one of those pulsing vines, soldier boy, while I whisper sweet nothings about open borders and sharing Tullius's gold with the beggars. Imperial Soldier: Watch your tongue, vampire whore—Empire don't tolerate your blood-sucking liberal drivel out here in the wilds. These runes on my armor?

Imperial Soldier: Open borders? With Stormcloaks crawling these woods like ticks on a skeever's ass? I'd sooner share my last Septim with that beast eyeing us from the brush—looks like it's got a hard-on for your vampire tits, lady. Keep your magic bullshit to yourself; Empire's holding this shithole together with steel, not spells.
Serana: Runes? I joined the Legion 'cause Daddy's farm was boring

**Late visual context as seen by MJR (the player): From MJR’s perspective, the scene feels steeped in quiet mystery. The forest ahead is dense, with towering trees whose trunks are almost shrouded in shadow, though faint rings of pale light seem to pulse faintly around their bases. A gravel path, worn and uneven, winds forward through the underbrush, leading the eye toward a small, mossy clearing where tufts of ferns and clusters of fungi burst from the earth. The air feels charged with stillness—until the eye lifts upward, where the sky bleeds into a dusk-like teal hue, streaked with ribbons of iridescent light that flicker like distant auroras. Dark, smoky clouds hover just beneath this glow, their edges tinged with black, as if some unseen force stirs unseen above. To the right, a steep rise of rocks and tangled roots looms, its surface barely visible under the canopy’s density, hinting at higher ground just out of reach. Everything is bathed in low, diffused light, sharp details softened by the gloom, yet MJR catches the way vines cling to tree bark and how the path’s gravel crunches underfoot—a sound that seems muted, as though swallowed by the earth.**

Serana: Runes? Oh honey, those squiggly dick-doodles on your tin-pot armor look like they were scratched in by a drunk Ayleid after a bad skooma binge—probably meant to ward off the likes of me sinking my fangs into your scrawny Imperial neck. But check this aurora bullshit dancing overhead; it's like the Divines are finger-banging the sky to spite Talos, all swirly green and blue like a hooker's bruised thighs after a rowdy night in Riften's alleys. Makes me wanna drag MJR under those glowing bastards for some open-air Daedric devotion, borders be damned—your Empire's walls can't stop the real invasions, like my tight little coven spilling seed across Skyrim's poor and needy.
Please wrap up the current topic between the NPCs in a natural way. Nobody is leaving, so there is no need for formal goodbyes.

Serana: ... it's like the gods are tripping balls on moon-sugar up there, painting the sky all purple and slutty just to light up these pines for a midnight fuck-fest. Those glowing little shits on the ground?
